skill.name: brand-landingpage
skill.description: Brand-first landing page designer. Runs a brand-identity interview to capture colors, typography, and shape language. Generates and iterates on a polished landing page via Stitch with deployment-ready HTML. Use when the user asks to create a landing page and has no established visual direction. Skip when they have a design mockup or need a dashboard or app UI.
skill.summary: Design branded landing pages through a guided brand interview and AI-powered visual generation via Stitch.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 295 potential issues but all are false positives or legitimate usage patterns. The 100+ 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are color hex values in design documentation misidentified as crypto hashes. The 142 'shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code block delimiters. The critical heuristic finding for 'dangerous combination' is dismissed: the skill checks for an API key env var (legitimate for Stitch integration), references documentation URLs, and uses markdown code fences. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected. Risk level is low.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API key
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI to verify an API key environment variable is set for the Stitch service. This is legitimate and expected for a skill that uses an external design API. The skill explicitly states 'Never display, transcribe, or echo the key' as a safeguard.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references two documentation URLs for the Stitch SDK. These are legitimate reference links, not data exfiltration endpoints. No credential data is sent to these URLs.

content.user_title: Design a branded landing page from a 5-minute interview
content.value_statement: Most landing page generators produce generic templates that do not reflect your product identity. This skill runs a focused brand interview then generates a custom, deployment-ready landing page that matches your specific brand feel, colors, and typography.
actual_capabilities: Run a structured brand interview to capture product identity and visual preferences
Create a Stitch design system with custom colors, fonts, and shape language
Generate and iterate on landing page designs through structured feedback loops
Save versioned HTML output and open it in the browser for full-fidelity review
Generate mobile-optimized variants after desktop approval
Deliver a deployment-ready bundle with index.html, design tokens, and a deploy checklist
limitations: Requires a Stitch API key and the Stitch SDK to be installed
Generates static HTML only, not React, Vue, or framework components
Cannot embed user-provided images or logos directly into the generated page
Limited to single-page landing sites, not multi-page applications or dashboards

use_cases.title: SaaS landing page from scratch
use_cases.description: A startup founder needs a landing page for their new SaaS product but has no brand guidelines. The skill runs an interview, establishes brand direction, and generates a polished page.

use_cases.title: Open source project homepage
use_cases.description: An open source maintainer wants a professional landing page for their GitHub project. The skill interviews for brand feel and generates a page with code snippets and social proof sections.

use_cases.title: Side project marketing page
use_cases.description: A developer built a side project and needs a quick landing page to share on social media. The skill quickly establishes visual direction and generates a deployment-ready page.
use_cases.target_user: Indie developers and side project creators

prompt_templates.title: Quick landing page from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a landing page for my new project. Can you help me design one?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user starting a new landing page project with no existing brand identity

prompt_templates.title: Landing page with existing brand
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a landing page for my SaaS product called DataFlow. We help teams monitor their data pipelines. Use our brand colors: primary hex #4F46E5, dark theme, clean sans-serif fonts.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who already knows their brand direction and wants to skip the full interview

prompt_templates.title: Iterate on an existing design
prompt_templates.prompt: The landing page looks good overall but the hero section feels flat. Can you make the headline more prominent and add more contrast to the CTA button?
prompt_templates.scenario: User giving design feedback after seeing the first generation

prompt_templates.title: Mobile variant generation
prompt_templates.prompt: The desktop version is approved. Can you generate a mobile layout too?
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user who wants a mobile-optimized version after desktop approval

output_examples.input: Create a landing page for my dev tool called Railtrack. It helps teams catch breaking changes before they deploy.
output_examples.output: The skill runs a brand interview covering product purpose, target users, brand adjectives, light or dark preference, color direction, font style, and shape language. After the interview it generates the first draft and opens it in your browser for review.

output_examples.input: The colors are too bright and it feels generic. Can you try a more muted palette and a bolder layout?
output_examples.output: The skill generates 2-3 variant options with adjusted color schemes and layout. Each variant is saved and opened in a separate browser tab for side-by-side comparison. The skill highlights one distinguishing feature per option.

The skill assembles a deployment-ready bundle containing index.html, mobile.html if generated, DESIGN.md with brand documentation, color-tokens.json, and a DEPLOY.md checklist. The bundle is zipped and ready for deployment to Vercel, Netlify, or GitHub Pages.

best_practices: Complete the full brand interview before generating. The interview takes 5 minutes and creates the difference between a generic template and a page that fits your brand.
Review the generated page in the browser by using the file the skill opens for you. Do not rely on text descriptions alone.
Save a list of your brand colors, preferred fonts, and target audience before starting. This makes the interview faster and the result more accurate.
anti_patterns: Skipping the brand interview to save time. Without the interview the skill generates a generic template that requires many rounds of revision.
Giving feedback in CSS terms like padding or font size. Instead describe the visual intent such as more breathing room or a bolder headline.
Providing image files or logos expecting them to be embedded. Stitch generates from text prompts only. The files are saved in the output bundle for manual addition.

faq.question: Do I need a Stitch account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill uses Stitch for visual generation. You need a Stitch API key and the Stitch SDK installed.

faq.question: How long does the interview take?
faq.answer: About 5 minutes. The skill asks a maximum of 10 questions across 3 phases covering product purpose, brand feel, and visual preferences.

faq.question: Can I use my own logo or images?
faq.answer: Stitch generates from text prompts and does not accept image uploads. Your logo is saved in the output bundle so you can add it to the HTML manually.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The skill generates static HTML files. The final bundle includes index.html, mobile.html if created, DESIGN.md, color tokens, and a deployment checklist.

faq.question: Can I edit the generated page after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. You can iterate through the skill's feedback loop for changes, or edit the final HTML directly after delivery.

faq.question: Does this skill support multi-page websites?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for single-page landing sites. It is not for multi-page applications, dashboards, or documentation sites.
